mission.title: jaeger: query error after run es_indices_clean.sh
mission.description: query error after run es_indices_clean.sh. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify jaeger troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your jaeger deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger -n observability --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
jaeger version: cba413ede300564e6f92a16f812604e4a829d0c7
storage: elasticsearch 6.1.1, Build: bd92e7f/2017-12-17T20:23:25.338Z, JVM: 9.0.1

It's work before clean.

steps.title: Apply the fix for query error after run es_indices_clean.sh
steps.description:
Instead of creating indices with mapping the collector at start time creates index template. ES uses this index template when a data is inserted to ES.

TODO
```yaml
⋊> ~/opt ./es_indices_clean.sh 0 localhost:9200                                                                                                                                                             
Installing python dependencies required for curator...
Requirement already satisfied: elasticsearch in /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
Requirement already satisfied: elasticsearch-curator in /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
Requirement already satisfied: urllib3<1.23,>=1.21.1 in /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages (from elasticsearch)
Requirement already satisfied: click>=6.7 
```

steps.title: Confirm query error after run es_indices_clean.sh is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger -n observability --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Instead of creating indices with mapping the collector at start time creates index template. ES uses this index template when a data is inserted to ES.

TODO

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
